frontHtml: What are the three zones of the prostate described by McNeal?
backHtml: Peripheral zone (70% of glandular tissue, most common site of carcinoma), Central zone (surrounds ejaculatory ducts), and Transition zone (site of BPH).

frontHtml: Name two classes of medications used for BPH and their mechanisms.
backHtml:
Alpha-1 blockers (tamsulosin, alfuzosin): relax smooth muscle (dynamic component).
5-alpha reductase inhibitors (finasteride, dutasteride): shrink prostate tissue (static component).
educationalObjective: List first-line medical therapies for BPH.
